per_query.text: Where was Gestalt psychology concieved?
per_query.answer_text:
Gestalt psychology was conceived in the Berlin School of Experimental Psychology. It aims to understand the laws governing our ability to acquire and maintain meaningful perceptions, emphasizing that the whole has a reality of its own, independent of its parts [0e94b4197b10096b1f4c699701570fbf::p2::c1]. 

Citations: [0e94b4197b10096b1f4c699701570fbf::p2::c1]

per_query.text: How does this document define the law of good gestalt?
per_query.answer_text:
The law of good gestalt is defined as the tendency for elements of objects to be perceptually grouped together if they form a pattern that is regular, simple, and orderly. This law suggests that individuals perceive the world by eliminating complexity and unfamiliarity, allowing them to observe reality in its simplest form. By reducing extraneous stimuli, the mind creates meaning, which implies a global regularity that is often prioritized over spatial relations. The law emphasizes conciseness, which is a foundational aspect of gestalt theory [0e94b4197b10096b1f4c699701570fbf::p11::c4].

Citations: [0e94b4197b10096b1f4c699701570fbf::p11::c4]

per_query.text: From the paper, which temperature gives ChatGPT the highest alignment score?
per_query.answer_text:
The temperature that gives ChatGPT the highest alignment score is 0.5, with a human average alignment score of 84.5 as reported in the evaluation results [2310.05634v2::p9::c46]. Other temperatures tested (0.1 and 0.9) did not yield higher scores than 0.5 [2310.05634v2::p6::c33].

per_query.text: For dataset construction, which step takes the most word to describe than the others.
per_query.answer_text:
The step that takes the most words to describe in the dataset construction process is "Evolutionary Question Generation." This step involves iteratively constructing paragraphs and questions about selected people, starting with a human-written sentence and applying a data selection algorithm to enhance the paragraph with additional knowledge from WikiData. The process is detailed and requires multiple iterations, making it more complex than the other steps, which are more straightforward in their descriptions, such as Person Selection and Name Disambiguation [2310.05634v2::p3::c12]. 

Citations: [2310.05634v2::p3::c12]

per_query.text: According to author's definition on conscious incompetence, when can a sentence map to both [NA] and a list of sub-graph knowledge?
per_query.answer_text:
A sentence can map to both [NA] and a list of sub-graph knowledge if it includes some knowledge that requires verification, which is absent in the knowledge graph G, while also being grounded by other knowledge that is present in G. This means that the sentence acknowledges a knowledge gap (represented by [NA]) but still references valid knowledge from the graph [2310.05634v2::p2::c9]. 

Citations: [2310.05634v2::p2::c9]

per_query.text: According to author's definition on conscious incompetence, when can a sentence map to either [NA] or a list of sub-graph knowledge?
per_query.answer_text:
A sentence can map to both [NA] and a list of sub-graph knowledge if it includes knowledge that requires verification, which is absent in the knowledge graph G, while also being partially verifiable by the knowledge graph itself. This means that the sentence acknowledges a knowledge gap (represented by [NA]) but still relates to some existing knowledge in the graph [2310.05634v2::p2::c9], [2310.05634v2::p3::c10]. 

Citations: [2310.05634v2::p2::c9], [2310.05634v2::p3::c10]

per_query.text: What is the performance of filter-then-rerank methods (w.o. ensemble) on 50-shot TACREV dataset?
per_query.answer_text:
The performance of filter-then-rerank methods (without ensemble) on the 50-shot TACREV dataset is reported as 70.8 (1.6) when using the Vicuna-13B model with reranking, and 72.3 (1.4) when using InstructGPT with reranking. The results indicate that the filter-then-rerank approach consistently improves performance across different settings, achieving significant gains compared to previous state-of-the-art methods [2303.08559v2::p9::c47], [2303.08559v2::p9::c48]. 

Citations: [2303.08559v2::p9::c47], [2303.08559v2::p9::c48]

per_query.text: What is the performance of filter-then-rerank methods (w.o. ensemble) on 50-shot CONLL03 dataset?
per_query.answer_text:
The performance of filter-then-rerank methods without ensemble on the 50-shot CONLL03 dataset shows an F1 score of 70.8 with a standard deviation of 1.6, as indicated in Table 3 of the provided context. This method consistently improves performance across various datasets and settings, demonstrating the effectiveness of the LLM reranking approach. 

Citations: [2303.08559v2::p8::c44], [2303.08559v2::p9::c47]

per_query.text: Tell me the prompt template used for label 'Contact.Meet' in filter-then-rerank method of this work.
per_query.answer_text:
The prompt template used for the label 'Contact.Meet' in the filter-then-rerank method is: "The word {evt} triggers a MEET event: two or more entities come together at a single location and interact with one another face-to-face." This template is designed to define the event type associated with the label clearly and concisely, facilitating the identification of relevant instances in the data.

per_query.answer_text:

This indicates that the event is triggered by the specific word {evt} which denotes a meeting between entities.

per_query.text: Among the adults conducted the survey on April 25 - May 1 2018, how many adults rated Trump's government ethical standards as poor?
per_query.answer_text:
Among the adults surveyed from April 25 to May 1, 2018, 36% rated the ethical standards of Trump's government as poor [05-03-18-political-release::p8::c19]. Additionally, 21% rated them as not good, making a total of 58% who viewed them negatively [05-03-18-political-release::p8::c19].

per_query.text: Among the adults conducted the survey on April 25 - May 1 2022, how many adults rated Trump's government ethical standards as poor?
per_query.answer_text:
Among the adults surveyed from April 25 to May 1, 2018, 36% rated the ethical standards of Trump's administration as poor. Additionally, 21% rated them as not good, leading to a total of 58% who viewed the ethical standards negatively. Among Democrats, 86% rated the administration's ethical standards as not good or poor, which includes 61% who rated them specifically as poor [05-03-18-political-release::p8::c19], [05-03-18-political-release::p9::c24].

Citations: [05-03-18-political-release::p8::c19], [05-03-18-political-release::p9::c24]

per_query.text: Who Visited the U.S. Naval Medical Research centre on November 29, 2012 in the document?
per_query.answer_text:
Rear Adm. (Ret.) Tim Ziemer, the U.S. Global Malaria Coordinator for the President’s Malaria Initiative, visited the U.S. Naval Medical Research Center on November 29, 2012. His visit included a briefing on current malaria vaccine research efforts and a tour of the facility, where he expressed interest in the malaria program at the laboratory. Capt. John Sanders, the NMRC commanding officer, provided an overview of the NMRC's infectious diseases research efforts, particularly in malaria [0b85477387a9d0cc33fca0f4becaa0e5::p1::c3], [0b85477387a9d0cc33fca0f4becaa0e5::p4::c14].

per_query.text: What is the average performance gap between the ProgramFC performance and our proposed system in the HOVER dataset? Please write down the answer in float format with 2 decimals.

The average performance gap between the ProgramFC performance and the proposed system (QACHECK) in the HOVER dataset is 10.38% for two-hop claims, 11.37% for three-hop claims, and 14.77% for four-hop claims. To find the average of these gaps: 

(10.38 + 11.37 + 14.77) / 3 = 12.17.

Thus, the average performance gap is 12.17.

per_query.text: How many USD dollars will the author spend if the annotators finish annotating all of claims of the authors' proposed dataset? Please write the answer in float format.
per_query.answer_text:
The author will spend a total of 87,026 claims annotated at a rate of 0.37 USD per claim. Therefore, the total cost will be calculated as follows:

Total cost = Total claims × Rate per claim = 87,026 × 0.37 = 32,193.62 USD.

Thus, the author will spend 32193.62 USD if the annotators finish annotating all claims of the proposed dataset.

Citations: [2305.13186v3::p4::c19]

per_query.text: How many claims are with the highest percentage of reasoning steps  in the author's proposed dataset?

The author's proposed dataset, SCITAB, contains claims that require up to 11 reasoning steps for verification, with 86% of the claims requiring 3 or more reasoning steps, indicating a high complexity of reasoning involved in the dataset [2305.13186v3::p4::c21], [2305.13186v3::p4::c23]. However, the exact number of claims with the highest percentage of reasoning steps is not stated in the provided context.

per_query.text: How many multi-head self-attention layers does DETR's default structure have?

per_query.text: In 2015, among all those that are considered as bigger threats to well-being of the United States compared to August 2014, which one has the biggest R-D difference?
per_query.answer_text:
The biggest R-D difference in perceived threats to the well-being of the United States in 2015, compared to August 2014, is related to global climate change, with a 51-point gap between Democrats (73%) and Republicans (22%) who view it as a major threat. This gap is noted as the largest measured since Pew Research began asking the question in 2009 [12-15-15-ISIS-and-terrorism-release-final::p16::c41].

per_query.text: although few says that current military effort in Iraq and Syria is going well,  what are the more people's attitude in still believe that the U.S. and its allies in the ISIS flight in the end in 2015?
per_query.answer_text:
Although few people believe that the current military effort in Iraq and Syria is going well, there is a significant level of optimism regarding the ultimate success of the U.S. and its allies in combating ISIS. Two-thirds (66%) of respondents believe that the U.S. and its allies will either definitely or probably succeed in their campaign against Islamic militants, which is an increase from 55% in July 2015. In contrast, 58% of people think the military effort is going either not too well or not at all well, while only 35% believe it is going fairly or very well [12-15-15-ISIS-and-terrorism-release-final::p18::c45], [12-15-15-ISIS-and-terrorism-release-final::p18::c44].

per_query.text: When using GPT-4 as the base language model, how many datasets does Logic-LM (without self-refinement) outperform the two baseline models in?
per_query.answer_text:
Logic-LM (without self-refinement) outperforms the two baseline models across all five datasets evaluated: ProofWriter, PrOntoQA, FOLIO, LogicalDeduction, and AR-LSAT. This is highlighted in the findings where it is stated that Logic-LM significantly outperforms standard LLMs and CoT across all datasets, achieving an average improvement of 39.2% over standard LLMs and 18.4% over CoT [2023.findings-emnlp.248::p7::c37], [2023.findings-emnlp.248::p1::c1].

per_query.text: In this paper, what symbolic solver is used to implement the logic programming module shown in Figure 1?
per_query.answer_text:
The symbolic solver used to implement the logic programming module shown in Figure 1 is the Pyke expert system. Pyke makes inferences based on the logic programming language and utilizes forward- and backward-chaining algorithms to derive new facts and substantiate goals in deductive reasoning tasks [2023.findings-emnlp.248::p5::c23]. 

Citations: [2023.findings-emnlp.248::p5::c23]

per_query.text: Which dataset used in this paper was proposed in 2022 and all of its logical reasoning problems are multiple-choice questions with 5 options?
per_query.answer_text:
The dataset proposed in 2022 that consists of logical reasoning problems formatted as multiple-choice questions with 5 options is FOLIO (Han et al., 2022). This dataset is noted for its challenging, expert-written problems that require complex first-order logic reasoning to solve, and it includes a total of 204 examples in its test set [2023.findings-emnlp.248::p6::c29]. 

Citations: [2023.findings-emnlp.248::p6::c29]

per_query.text: Which type of semantic errors has the lowest total percentages in HOVER dataset?

This is lower than other categories such as Token, Structure, and Incorrect execution, which have higher percentages across the different hop levels [2023.acl-long.386::p8::c36].

This is lower than other categories such as "Token" (8%), "Structure" (19%), and "Semantic error" (29%) for 2-hop claims, as shown in the error analysis table [2023.acl-long.386::p8::c36].

per_query.text: According to the figure, what is the first step of cold start?
per_query.answer_text:
The first step of the cold start process is to collect in-context examples by prompting ChatGPT solely through system messages and visual annotations, using a heuristic approach. This stage continues until satisfactory in-context examples are identified, which are crucial for generating high-quality instruction-response pairs later in the pipeline [2306.05425v1::p5::c21].

per_query.text: What literature is shown in the visual examples of MIMIC-IT and Otter?
per_query.answer_text:
The visual examples of MIMIC-IT include a comprehensive collection of videos and sequential images in an egocentric view, derived from the IEP and E4D scenarios. The IEP scenario focuses on understanding and planning within indoor environments, while the E4D scenario tailors instructions for first-person augmented reality (AR) headset applications. These datasets enhance the model's ability to perceive scenes from a first-person viewpoint and provide insights for AR headset users, as illustrated in the visual examples of Otter's capabilities in Fig. 5 [2306.05425v1::p9::c41], [2306.05425v1::p8::c34].

Citations: [2306.05425v1::p9::c41], [2306.05425v1::p8::c34]

per_query.answer_text:
The in-context examples for the multi-lingual translation query include translations into 7 additional languages: Chinese, Japanese, Spanish, German, French, Korean, and Arabic, in addition to English, making a total of 8 languages supported for multi-modal instruction-response pairs [2306.05425v1::p15::c70], [2306.05425v1::p5::c19]. 

Citations: [2306.05425v1::p15::c70], [2306.05425v1::p5::c19]

per_query.text: How many strategies outperform the random selection baseline on CIFAR-10-LT?
per_query.answer_text:
On CIFAR-10-LT, the hard-to-contrast strategy significantly outperforms the random selection baseline by 21.2% and 24.1% when querying 20% and 30% of the entire dataset, respectively. This indicates that at least one strategy, hard-to-contrast, clearly outperforms the random selection baseline in this context. Other strategies like easy-to-learn and hard-to-learn are mentioned but do not perform as effectively without ground truths, which are not available in active learning settings [2210.02442v1::p7::c34].

Citations: [2210.02442v1::p7::c34]

per_query.text: How many strategies outperform the uniform sampling baseline on CIFAR-10-LT?
per_query.answer_text:
On CIFAR-10-LT, multiple strategies outperform the uniform sampling baseline. Specifically, the hard-to-contrast strategy significantly outperforms random selection by 21.2% and 24.1% when querying 20% and 30% of the dataset, respectively [2210.02442v1::p7::c34]. Additionally, other strategies such as BALD, Consistency, Margin, VAAL, and Coreset also yield better performance compared to the uniform sampling baseline [2210.02442v1::p19::c75]. 

Citations: [2210.02442v1::p7::c34], [2210.02442v1::p19::c75]

per_query.text: What querying criteria yields the worst label diversity generally on all the datasets?
per_query.answer_text:
The querying criteria that yields the worst label diversity generally across all datasets is "Random." This is highlighted in the context where it is stated that most active querying strategies exhibit a selection bias towards specific classes, resulting in poor class coverage, especially at low budgets. In contrast, random sampling can provide a more balanced class distribution, which is often not achieved by other active querying strategies like Entropy, Margin, and VAAL, particularly at the beginning of active learning [2210.02442v1::p2::c5], [2210.02442v1::p7::c32]. 

Citations: [2210.02442v1::p2::c5], [2210.02442v1::p7::c32]

The paper contains multiple line plots, specifically referenced in the context of gas price comparisons. There are at least three distinct line plots mentioned: one for the years 2015 to 2018, another for 2016 to 2019, and a third for 2019 to 2023. Additionally, the performance comparisons for reasoning over complex line plots are illustrated in Figures 11-13, indicating further line plots are discussed in the paper. 

Citations: [2309.17421v2::p25::c90], [2309.17421v2::p24::c88], [2309.17421v2::p19::c75], [2309.17421v2::p19::c76]

per_query.text: What are the optimizers used in this research?
per_query.answer_text:
The research utilizes the AdamW optimizer for training, with specific hyperparameters including β1 = 0.9, β2 = 0.95, and eps = 10−5, along with a weight decay of 0.1 and gradient clipping of 1.0. Additionally, a constant learning rate of 10−6 is applied during training. For some models, a KL penalty is also set, with values of β = 0.01 for the 7B and 13B models, and β = 0.005 for the 34B and 70B models. In contrast, other parts of the research mention the use of SGD as an optimizer for different settings, such as in the training of ResNet-50 models [2307.09288v2::p15::c86], [2307.09288v2::p5::c25], [2210.02442v1::p14::c63].

Citations: [2307.09288v2::p15::c86], [2307.09288v2::p5::c25], [2210.02442v1::p14::c63]

per_query.text: What are the large language models used in this research?
per_query.answer_text:
The large language models used in this research include Codex, InstructGPT, GPT-3, GPT-4, and LLaMA. Codex is mentioned as the model whose API is used for inference, while InstructGPT is noted for its reliance on external APIs in the QACHECK system. Additionally, GPT-3 and GPT-4 are referenced in the context of their capabilities and energy consumption, and LLaMA is highlighted as an open-source model being considered for future integration into the system [2023.acl-long.386::p9::c45], [2307.09288v2::p35::c179], [2310.07609v1::p7::c32], [2023.findings-emnlp.248::p10::c55].

Citations: [2023.acl-long.386::p9::c45], [2307.09288v2::p35::c179], [2310.07609v1::p7::c32], [2023.findings-emnlp.248::p10::c55]

per_query.text: Does the mean significance of information flow from the text part to label words always greater than the mean significance of information flow from label words to the target position? Answer 'yes' or 'no' directly.
per_query.answer_text:
No, the mean significance of information flow from the text part to label words (Swp) does not always exceed the mean significance of information flow from label words to the target position (Spq). In shallow layers, Swp is high while Spq is low, but in deep layers, Spq becomes the dominant one, indicating that the significance of information flow from label words to the target position increases as layers deepen [2305.14160v4::p3::c12]. 

Citations: [2305.14160v4::p3::c12]

per_query.text: Does the  information flow from label words to the target position dominate in all layers? Answer 'yes' or 'no' directly.
per_query.answer_text:
No, the information flow from label words to the target position does not dominate in all layers. In shallow layers, the significance of this information flow (Spq) is low, while in deep layers, it becomes the dominant one. This indicates that the importance of information flow from label words increases as the layers go deeper, but it is not dominant in shallow layers [2305.14160v4::p3::c12], [2305.14160v4::p3::c13].

Citations: [2305.14160v4::p3::c12], [2305.14160v4::p3::c13]

per_query.text: Does randomly isolating non-label words within the last 5 layers have almost no impact? Answer 'yes' or 'no' directly.
per_query.answer_text:
Yes, randomly isolating non-label words within the last 5 layers has almost no impact on the model's behavior. This is highlighted by the observation that the influence of isolating label words becomes inconsequential within the last 5 layers, or when random non-label words are used, emphasizing the importance of shallow-layer information aggregation via label words in in-context learning (ICL) [2305.14160v4::p4::c20].

Citations: [2305.14160v4::p4::c20]

per_query.text: How much does the Anchor Re-weighting method improve the performance of vanilla ICL on average?
per_query.answer_text:
The Anchor Re-weighting method improves the performance of vanilla In-Context Learning (ICL) by an average of 16.7% in accuracy compared to standard ICL baselines [2305.14160v4::p2::c6]. This method adjusts the significance of different label words in demonstrations, leading to significant enhancements in model performance across various datasets [2305.14160v4::p6::c33]. 

Citations: [2305.14160v4::p2::c6], [2305.14160v4::p6::c33]

per_query.text: Using anchor-only context compression, how much can GPT2-XL speed up on AGNews?
per_query.answer_text:
Using anchor-only context compression, GPT2-XL achieves a speed-up of 2.5× on the AGNews dataset, which has the longest demonstration length among the datasets analyzed. This indicates that the efficiency of the Hiddenanchor method is particularly pronounced in contexts with longer demonstration lengths, leading to higher acceleration ratios [2305.14160v4::p8::c48], [2305.14160v4::p14::c92]. 

Citations: [2305.14160v4::p8::c48], [2305.14160v4::p14::c92]
